title: BA 210 - Legal Environment of Business
description: Judicial system, constitution, administrative agencies, torts, crimes, creation and performance of contracts, sales, consumer protection, commercial paper, and business ethics. Law case studies.

title: BA 250 - Financial Accounting
description: First course in accounting. Measurement and reporting principles used in US to prepare financial reports for investors/creditors. Computer applications demonstrate design of accounting information systems.

title: BA 252 - Management Accounting
description: Introduction to accounting information system used for internal decision making within organizations, which include planning, operational control, and performance evaluation.

title: ECON 202 - Principles of Microeconomics
description: Explore markets and how individuals and businesses make economic decisions. Topics include scarcity, supply/demand, market equilibrium, elasticity, consumer theory, production, costs, & profits under different market structures, factor markets, and market failure.

title: ECON 203 - Principles of Macroeconomics
description: Analysis of the economy as a whole, including aggregate supply/demand, business cycles, GDP, inflation, unemployment, financial institutions, interest rates, monetary and fiscal policy, international trade/finance, and economic growth.

title: MATH 104 - Finite Mathematics
description: Topics include linear models, systems of linear equations, linear programming with two variables, financial mathematics, sets, basic probability and an introduction to descriptive statistics.

title: MATH 104I - Finite Mathematics with Integrated Support
description: Integrated support for development of quantitative reasoning through business-relevant topics including linear models, systems of linear equations, linear programming, financial mathematics, sets, basic probability and descriptive statistics. Open to students in Math placement category III or IV.

title: BA 322 - Business Analytics
description: Explore basic data analytics concepts, techniques and tools used in the process of data-driven business decision-making and strategy, drawing real examples from different functional areas in business.

title: BA 340 - Principles of Marketing
description: Domestic and international institutions, concepts, and management processes in marketing. Marketing research. Simulations, cases, exercises. Computer applications.

title: BA 360 - Principles of Finance
description: Basic skills for analyzing financial data. Time value of money; techniques and ratios commonly used in financial analysis.

title: BA 370 - Principles of Management
description: Theory, behavior, production and operations, and interpersonal communication in organizations: large or small, profit or nonprofit, domestic or international.

title: BA 496 - Strategic Management
description: Capstone course integrating all business core courses into design of strategic business plans. Domestic/international cases. Simulations and projects. Micro/mainframe computer applications.

title: BA 106 - Advocating for Sustainability
description: Provides an understanding of sustainability issues, discovering what you are passionate about and modifying behaviors regarding consumption, work, living decisions, etc. to become well-informed strong advocates for change.

title: BA 106D - Advocating for Sustainability - Additional Depth
description: Additional depth by analyzing sustainability initiatives/reporting in businesses.

title: BA 120 - Business Essentials
description: Orientation to the Business Program. Develops a core set of skills and exposure to business functional areas: accounting, finance, management, marketing, ethics, and strategy.

title: BA 304 - Business Psychology
description: Critically examines the psychological foundation of business by looking at how business agents think, feel and act in various situations and how managers make decisions.

title: BA 430 - Introduction to Entrepreneurship
description: Planning, start up, sources of capital, location, form, budgeting, record keeping, marketing, management.

title: BA 438 - Applications in Entrepreneurship
description: Study entrepreneurial strategy and implementation using contemporary forms of business planning.

title: BA 439 - New Venture Consulting
description: Complete a consulting project and business plan with a new venture. Class meetings and field work each week.

title: BA 441 - Retailing and Services Marketing
description: Following a case, discussion, and hands-on format, the course introduces students to retailing in a technology-rich environment and marketing from a service-dominant perspective.

title: BA 445 - Marketing Communications
description: Comprehensive examination of marketing communications activity and its environment; topics discussed include targeting, positioning, objectives setting, budgeting, sales promotion, personal selling, advertising, and public relations.

title: BA 446 - Marketing Research
description: Study and application of primary and secondary marketing research through group work or local organizations. Activities include survey design and execution, data entry and analysis, report preparation and presentation.

title: BA 447 - E-Commerce / E-Marketing Strategy
description: Through interactive discussions, group activities and individual assignments, students will explore e-commerce and e-marketing concepts to understand how Internet has altered buying and selling of goods and services today.

title: BA 448 - Consumer Behavior
description: Study of how organizations design and modify marketing strategies by understanding changing consumer lifestyles and attitudes in a multicultural world, and the resulting consumer behaviors in the global marketplace.

title: BA 449 - Macromarketing
description: Examines the relationship between marketing and society. Topics include technology, ethics, materialism, globalization, sustainability. The course is multicultural and multidisciplinary.
